board.title: Open Source Basics
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: The tools we use today come from decades of open source work. We stand on the shoulders of giants.

clues.question: Build file for the make tool, created at Bell Labs to manage dependencies; requires tabs, not spaces
Extensible object-relational database system developed at UC Berkeley; its creator later won the Turing Award
Modular open-source HTTP server; “a patchy server” was an early pun on its name
Distributed version control system with full local commit history; originally created for kernel development
GNU Compiler Collection, originally the GNU C Compiler
Recursive acronym for a free Unix alternative; “___’s Not Unix”
Unix command-line interpreter; “Bourne Again Shell,” successor to the Bourne shell
Open-source TLS/SSL cryptography library used for HTTPS
Terminal text editor scripted with Vimscript; one side of the editor wars
Permissive software license named after a Cambridge university
Open-source implementation of the Secure Shell (SSH) protocol
Extensible text editor written in Elisp; the other side of the editor wars
